item_name: Lottie Creation Tools — Lottielab, LottieFiles, AE+Bodymovin
definition_origin: Lottie is an open JSON-based vector animation format (originally Bodymovin AE plugin, 2017) that ships small, scriptable, infinitely scalable animations to native iOS, Android, React Native, and the web. 'Lottie creation tools' are the authoring environments that produce that JSON (or its newer dotLottie zipped variant) — historically After Effects + Bodymovin, increasingly Lottielab, LottieFiles' web editor, Jitter, Phase, and SVGator.
core_purpose: Replaces heavy GIF/MP4 micro-interactions and frame-by-frame mascot loops with vector animations that are tiny (1-50 KB), runtime-controllable (seek, pause, color-tint, loop segments), and crisp on Retina/AMOLED displays. On Norman's tripartite model: Visceral (smooth springy onboarding sparkles, brand mascot wave), Behavioral (success checkmarks, loading states, empty-state delight that confirms an action worked), and Reflective (signature looping logos that stick in memory and become brand shorthand).
when_needed: Consumer mobile: nearly always — onboarding, empty states, success/error toasts, loading, brand signature animations. B2B/dev-tools: more sparingly — empty states, illustration headers in marketing pages, micro-feedback (subscribe, copy-success). AI-wrapper: thinking/streaming indicators and 'magic generating' loops are signature uses. Marketing: hero animations, scroll-triggered illustration reveals, feature grid loops.
format_options: JSON (.json) Lottie: raw text, human-readable, editable by hand, universally supported by lottie-web, lottie-react-native, lottie-ios, lottie-android. Larger file size when images/fonts are embedded. dotLottie (.lottie): ZIP archive of one-or-more Lottie JSONs plus image/font assets, Deflate-compressed. 50–70% smaller than equivalent JSON (one community example: 1000 KB JSON → 80 KB dotLottie). Supported by dotlottie-player, lottie-react v3+, recent lottie-web, Webflow, Framer, Lottielab. GIF/MP4/WebP fallbacks: export when target runtime can't load Lottie (email, legacy embeds, social posts). Rive (.riv): not Lottie but adjacent — when you need state machines, runtime data binding, scripting (see Item 19). SVG/SMIL: only for ultra-simple loops; no React Native parity. Recommendation: ship dotLottie as primary, JSON as compatibility fallback, MP4 only for marketing video.
file_size_performance: Target 2–10 KB JSON / 1–5 KB dotLottie for icon-sized loops, ≤50 KB JSON / ≤25 KB dotLottie for illustrative scenes, ≤200 KB for full hero animations. Optimization tactics: replace embedded raster images with vectors, simplify Bezier paths (LottieFiles Optimizer cuts 30–60%), drop unused expressions, lower keyframe count, prefer shape layers over masks. Render budget: keep simultaneous Lottie instances <3 on a mobile screen; use lottie-react-native's renderMode='HARDWARE' on Android; on iOS use the native CALayer renderer. Avoid per-frame text rendering — convert text to outlines.
free_tools: Lottielab (free tier: unlimited public projects, web-based, no AE required, exports JSON/dotLottie/GIF/MP4, real-time collaboration, paid tiers $12-15/mo for private projects + brand kit). LottieFiles Web Editor (free with account, integrated marketplace of 200k+ free Lotties, AE plugin, dotLottie support, Figma plugin). LottieFiles AE Plugin (free, works in After Effects, preview-on-device feature, supersedes raw Bodymovin for most users). Bodymovin (free open-source AE plugin, the original Lottie exporter, still the most exhaustive feature parity with AE). Jitter (free tier: unlimited projects, watermark-free, exports Lottie/MP4/GIF, web-based, Figma-like UX, paid Pro $16/mo for advanced features). Phase (free during beta, web-based motion design with Figma-like keyframe interface). SVGator (free tier: 3 projects, 6 export/mo, exports Lottie/SVG/MP4, paid $9-18/mo). Rive (free editor, exports Lottie as well as .riv — see Item 19). Haiku Animator (free tier discontinued — note as deprecated).
paid_tools: Adobe After Effects + Bodymovin/LottieFiles plugin ($22.99/mo standalone or $59.99/mo Creative Cloud All Apps): the ceiling — every expression, every effect, every shape layer feature works. Worth it when you do 5+ animations/month, need character-rig animation, or already own AE for video. Lottielab Pro ($12-15/seat/mo): worth it when you need private projects, version history, brand kits, and team collaboration without AE skill. Jitter Pro ($16/mo): worth it for marketing-heavy teams that need rapid Figma→animated-export workflows. Tiering: starter $9-15/mo (Lottielab/Jitter/SVGator) — sufficient for solo dev shipping <20 animations/year. Pro $20-30/mo (AE Single App or Lottielab Team) — for committed motion practice. Enterprise $50+/mo (Adobe CC All Apps + LottieFiles Teams) — only when crossing into video, illustration, or multi-creator workflows.
gemini_pro_workflow: Step 1 — Imagen 4 (in Whisk or Gemini app) generates the still illustration concepts: 'flat vector illustration of a cute fox waving, single-line stroke, soft pastel palette #FFE5D9 #F4A582, isolated on transparent background, behance trending mascot style'. Generate 6-12 variants, pick best stylistic direction. Step 2 — Use Gemini Pro multimodal to extract palette + describe shapes: paste the chosen image and ask 'Break this illustration into named shape layers with hex colors and approximate Bezier path coordinates suitable for an SVG'. Step 3 — Pipe Gemini's SVG into Lottielab via paste-as-SVG; rebuild as native shape layers (avoid embedded raster). Step 4 — For motion ideation, prompt Gemini Pro with 'Suggest a 12-keyframe animation script for a fox-waving Lottie: timing in milliseconds, easing per layer, secondary motion on tail and ears'. Step 5 — Veo (if available) for reference video of motion intent the Lottie should mimic — feed the video as inspiration, not as the source. Gems use case: build a custom 'Lottie Animator' Gem with system prompt 'You are a motion designer. Given an SVG and a brand archetype, output JSON keyframe specs for Lottielab in plain English'.
workflow_steps: 1. Concept (15 min): brand archetype + emotion target + reference Lotties from LottieFiles search. 2. Static design (45 min): Imagen 4 generates 6-12 illustration variants in your brand palette → pick one, redraw as clean SVG in Figma or trace via Vectorizer.AI. 3. Import to editor (10 min): paste SVG into Lottielab, layer-name everything (Body, Tail, EyeLeft, EyeRight). 4. Block timing (30 min): set keyframes at 0s, 0.5s, 1s with major poses; preview at 60fps. 5. Add secondary motion (45 min): tail swing, ear bounce, pupil tracking — ease-in-out + 3-frame anticipation. 6. Polish easing (20 min): swap default ease for spring or custom Bezier (cubic-bezier(0.34, 1.56, 0.64, 1) for friendly bounce). 7. Optimize (10 min): run LottieFiles Optimizer, strip unused layers, target dotLottie ≤25 KB. 8. Export both formats (5 min): JSON + dotLottie. 9. Integrate (15 min): drop into RN with lottie-react-native, web with @lottiefiles/dotlottie-react. 10. Test reduced-motion (10 min): add prefers-reduced-motion fallback to a static PNG. Total first-time: ~3.5 hr; experienced: 45 min.
ai_prompt_templates: Imagen/Whisk illustration prompt: 'Flat 2D vector mascot illustration of {character} {action}, {brand_palette}, single-weight outline, no gradients, isolated on transparent, suitable for Lottie animation, clean shape geometry, {archetype_modifier} mood, behance editorial illustration style, 1024x1024.' Gemini motion-script prompt: 'You are a Lottie motion designer. For this SVG illustration of {character}, output a 12-frame keyframe table at 24fps. Columns: layer_name, time_ms, transform (x, y, rotation, scale), easing. Animation should convey {emotion} and last {duration}s. Apply Disney 12-principles: anticipation 3 frames before main action, follow-through 4 frames after.' Gemini critique prompt: 'Critique this Lottie storyboard for AI-slop signs: gradient blobs, generic chibi, mismatched ease-curves, missed anticipation. Suggest 3 specific revisions with exact frame numbers.' Refinement prompt: 'Re-export the keyframe JSON with a spring easing of {stiffness:200, damping:18, mass:1} on the {layer} layer for the bounce effect.'
claude_code_integration: Install: pnpm add lottie-react-native (for RN) or pnpm add @lottiefiles/dotlottie-react (for web/RN with dotLottie). Drop assets in apps/mobile/src/assets/lottie/ for mobile, apps/web/public/lottie/ for web. Claude Code can: (1) auto-generate a typed wrapper component '<BrandLottie name="fox-wave" loop autoplay />' that handles reduced-motion fallback and sizes; (2) write a build-time optimizer script (scripts/optimize-lottie.mjs) using @lottiefiles/lottie-optimizer to crunch all .json files in CI; (3) generate Storybook stories for each animation; (4) lint for embedded raster images via JSON parse + check for 'image' assets > 0; (5) emit prefers-reduced-motion CSS/RN-equivalent fallbacks automatically.
emotional_design_integration: Caregiver archetype (Calm, Headspace): slow, soft-spring eases, 1.2-2s loops, soothing organic shapes, never-jarring transitions, breathing-rhythm timing. Magician (Linear, Arc): rapid morphs, particle systems, glowing trails, 200-400ms snappy transformations that feel like creation-from-nothing. Jester (Duolingo, Discord): rubber-band overshoot, exaggerated squash-and-stretch, expressive blinking and tail-wagging, faces with personality. Sage (Stripe, Notion docs): minimal, precise, single-stroke icons that draw themselves on, no character animation — just elegant lines. Hero (Strava, Nike): triumphant rise-and-settle, bold strokes, confident eases. The Lottie itself becomes the archetype proof — a Caregiver app with snappy spring eases reads as off-brand instantly.
specific_examples: Headspace (https://lottiefiles.com/featured-artists/headspace) — soft breathing-pulse loops on meditation timers, 100% Caregiver archetype, Lotties drive the entire emotional tempo. Duolingo (https://lottiefiles.com/featured-artists/duolingo) — Duo the owl's celebration, sad, and 'streak saved' Lotties carry the Jester archetype across iOS/Android/web with single source of truth. Calm — wave and forest-loop Lotties on sleep stories, ambient looping motion. Notion — empty-state Lottie illustrations (https://www.notion.so) on database first-use, simple, friendly, never overwhelming. Linear — extremely subtle UI Lotties (issue-status morphs, success ticks) reinforce the 'craft' archetype. Stripe — payment-success animations on checkout pages, restrained, premium. Airbnb — original Lottie use case (host-onboarding sparkle, the original Bodymovin proof-of-concept).
anti_ai_slop_traps: (1) Imagen-generated 'cute mascot' with melted hands or six-fingered paws — always redraw as SVG; never trace AI raster directly. (2) Generic chibi-style fox/cat/owl that looks identical across 50 indie apps — give your character a distinguishing silhouette feature (asymmetric ear, stripe pattern, accessory). (3) Default Bodymovin ease-curves (linear or stock ease-in-out) — replace with cubic-bezier(0.34, 1.56, 0.64, 1) friendly-spring or platform-native springs. (4) Glassmorphic gradient blobs floating behind the character — pure 2024 AI-slop signal. (5) 'Looping eternally with no rest' — real animations breathe; add a 200-400ms hold at start/end. (6) Misaligned anchor points causing the character to drift unintentionally — set anchor to character's center-of-gravity. (7) Embedded JPGs in the Lottie file (file balloons to 1MB) — convert to vectors. (8) Emoji-style faces with pupils that don't track — pupils should anticipate motion. Counter-prompt strategies: append 'no gradient mesh, no glassmorphism, no generic chibi, distinctive silhouette, single-weight stroke, off-center asymmetric features, behance editorial mascot style' to every Imagen prompt.
common_pitfalls: Performance: stacking 5+ Lotties on one screen causes jank on low-end Android — prefer one large hero animation per route. Accessibility: forgetting prefers-reduced-motion fallback (Lottie autoplay loops can trigger vestibular disorders). Consistency: each animation hand-tuned by a different prompt produces 5 mismatched motion personalities — define a 'brand motion spec' (ease curves, timing, breathing rhythm) before producing any. Scope creep: solo devs spend 8 hours perfecting a 2-second loop nobody notices — set a 90-min budget per micro-animation. File-size assumption: assuming JSON is fine; not migrating to dotLottie loses 50-70% in payload. Caching: not lazy-loading off-screen Lotties (lottie-react-native renders even when not visible by default). Color theming: hard-coding hex colors in JSON breaks dark-mode — use Lottie's color override at runtime instead.
accessibility_notes: Always provide accessibilityLabel on the Lottie component ('Loading animation showing a fox running'). Honor prefers-reduced-motion: render a static PNG (export the Lottie's first frame as PNG fallback). Avoid >3Hz flashing in any Lottie (WCAG 2.3.1 seizure threshold). For looping decorative Lotties, set aria-hidden='true' on the web. Provide a pause control for animations >5s. Color-not-only-signal: success checkmark animation must include a check shape, not just green-flash.
mobile_vs_web_diffs: Mobile (RN): lottie-react-native uses native CALayer/Drawable renderers — generally smoother but with renderer-version skew between iOS and Android (test both). dotLottie support requires v6.7+. Asset bundling: include in Hermes/Metro bundler via require(). Web: lottie-web uses SVG/Canvas/WebGL renderers; dotLottie via @lottiefiles/dotlottie-web. CDN-cacheable, can lazy-load via dynamic import. iOS-only quirk: AE expressions don't all translate — test on lottie-ios. Android-only quirk: shadow rendering and gradient strokes can flicker — use 'HARDWARE' renderMode.
sources: [LottieFiles vs Bodymovin official comparison](https://lottiefiles.com/blog/working-with-lottie-animations/bodymovin-or-lottiefiles-for-after-effects); [Lottielab AE alternative](https://www.lottielab.com/compare/after-effects-alternative); [11 Lottie creation tools](https://creattie.com/blog/Tools-for-Creating-Lottie-Animations); [dotLottie format overview](https://dotlottie.io/intro/); [Introducing dotLottie (LottieFiles)](https://lottiefiles.com/blog/working-with-lottie-animations/introducing-dotlottie-open-source-file-format); [Lottie Wikipedia entry](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lottie_(file_format)); [Bodymovin alternatives (Medium, Irina Kegishyan)](https://irina-kegishyan.medium.com/bodymovin-alternatives-expanding-your-lottie-animation-toolkit-4e6e3ffcbaac); [Top 10 Free LottieFiles alternatives 2025](https://www.femaleswitch.com/directories/tpost/pv08lg18n1-top-10-free-alternatives-to-lottiefiles)
